project: Influx v1 — arXiv Fetcher + Filter Pipeline + Canonical Note Render + Archive
description: Deliver the arXiv side of the Influx v1 pipeline end-to-end: Atom-feed fetcher against `export.arxiv.org` with client-side date filtering and per-category min-interval pacing; LLM relevance scoring in JSON mode with Pydantic validation and per-profile threshold tagging (relevance / full_text / deep_extract / notify_immediate); canonical Lithos note render+parse with exact-byte `## User Notes` preservation, the `## Archive` section contract, Influx-owned tag replacement, `profile:*` union merging, and preservation of external tags; PDF archive download via the guarded HTTP client from PRD 02 with path-safety verification and the failure path that emits `influx:repair-needed` + `influx:archive-missing` tags; and a real `run_profile` wire-up that calls arXiv → filter → (per accepted item) archive + render note → stubbed `lithos_client.write`. Real MCP calls, the repair sweep, HTML/PDF/article extraction, Tier 2/3 enrichment, webhook notifier, and RSS fetcher are out of scope and remain stubs or seams — `lithos_client.write`, `lithos_client.cache_lookup`, and `enrich.tier1_enrich` are documented stubs replaced by PRDs 05 and 07.

userStories.title: Create urls.py with canonical URL normalisation helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a pure URL normalisation helper (in `src/influx/urls.py` or `src/influx/notes.py`, per PRD 04 §5.5) that is reused by both the note renderer and the forthcoming MCP layer (PRD 05), so that arXiv and other sources agree on a single canonical `source_url` shape (FR-MCP-4, FR-MCP-5).
acceptanceCriteria: A pure URL normalisation function (exact name, signature, and module location — `src/influx/urls.py` or `src/influx/notes.py` — left to the implementation per FR-MCP-4) lowercases scheme and host, drops default ports (80 for http, 443 for https), strips `utm_*`, `fbclid`, `gclid`, `mc_cid`, `mc_eid`, and `ref` query parameters, removes a trailing slash on the path, and preserves the fragment verbatim (FR-MCP-4)
An arXiv-specific helper (or documented convention) constructs the canonical arXiv URL `https://arxiv.org/abs/{id}` from a raw arXiv ID (FR-MCP-5)

tests/unit/test_url_normalisation.py covers AC-04-H: `https://arXiv.org:443/abs/2601.12345?utm_source=twitter` normalises to `https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.12345`, and a URL with a `#foo` fragment preserves the fragment

tests/unit/test_url_normalisation.py also covers: mixed-case host lowercased, default port 80 on http stripped, non-default ports (e.g. 8443) preserved, multiple tracking params stripped while unrelated params preserved, trailing slash on path removed, and fragment preserved when query is stripped
The normaliser leaves unrelated query parameters untouched (only the documented tracking-param allowlist is stripped)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create schemas.py with FilterResponse and FilterResult Pydantic models
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/schemas.py` to define the Pydantic models used by the filter pipeline so that LLM JSON-mode responses are strictly validated with bounded score/tag lengths (FR-FLT-3).
acceptanceCriteria: src/influx/schemas.py defines `FilterResult` with fields `id: str`, `score: int` constrained to `1..10` inclusive, `tags: list[str]` constrained to length `0..5` inclusive, and `reason: str` (FR-FLT-3)
src/influx/schemas.py defines `FilterResponse` with field `results: list[FilterResult]` (FR-FLT-3)

Validation errors are raised (Pydantic ValidationError) when score is out of `[1,10]`, tags list exceeds length 5, or required fields are missing — a unit test in tests/unit/test_filter.py (or a dedicated tests/unit/test_schemas.py) asserts each negative case
A positive unit test confirms a well-formed JSON payload parses into `FilterResponse` with correct field types
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create lithos_client.py and enrich.py stub modules with documented replacement notes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/lithos_client.py` and `src/influx/enrich.py` as documented stubs so that the arXiv flow can call `lithos_client.write`, `lithos_client.cache_lookup`, and `enrich.tier1_enrich` during this PRD while PRDs 05 and 07 swap in real implementations (Definition of Done items in §10 of PRD 04, §4 internal seams).
acceptanceCriteria: src/influx/lithos_client.py exposes `write(...)` that records every call (arguments retained in-process for test assertions) and `cache_lookup(...)` that always returns a "not cached" result (exact API shape and recording mechanism left to the implementation)
src/influx/lithos_client.py module docstring explicitly says "replaced by PRD 05"

src/influx/enrich.py exposes `tier1_enrich(...)` that returns a canned Tier-1-shape result derived from the provided abstract (exact return shape left to the implementation but must be usable by the note renderer's `## Summary` section)
src/influx/enrich.py module docstring explicitly says "replaced by PRD 07"

A unit test confirms `lithos_client.write` records its calls in a retrievable way and `lithos_client.cache_lookup` returns a "not cached" result, and that `enrich.tier1_enrich` returns a non-empty Tier-1 shape for a sample abstract
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement notes.py parser for frontmatter, title, sections, and User Notes region
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/notes.py` to expose a parser that splits an existing canonical note into frontmatter, title, Influx-owned body sections, and the `## User Notes` region (everything below the heading) so that rewrites can preserve user content byte-identically (FR-NOTE-1, FR-NOTE-4).
acceptanceCriteria: src/influx/notes.py exposes a parser (exact API shape and return representation left to the implementation) that, given a note's text, recovers enough state to support the downstream rewrite pipeline: frontmatter (including tags), title, the Influx-owned body sections encountered above `## User Notes` (generic section capture — the concrete `## Archive` parse contract is owned by US-006), and the `## User Notes` region preserved byte-exactly (FR-NOTE-1, FR-NOTE-4)
When `## User Notes` is absent, the parser signals that absence (e.g. via a dedicated field or sentinel) so the renderer can append an empty one on rewrite (FR-NOTE-4)
The parser preserves the `## User Notes` region byte-identically — no whitespace stripping, no line-ending normalisation, no trimming
The parser is tolerant of notes that contain only frontmatter + title + `## User Notes` (no Influx-owned body sections yet)

tests/unit/test_notes_parse.py covers: well-formed note with all sections; note missing `## User Notes`; note with only title + `## User Notes`; note whose `## User Notes` region contains blank lines, unicode, and nested markdown headings — all preserved byte-exactly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement notes.py tag-merging rules for rewrite (FR-NOTE-5/6/7/8)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/notes.py` to expose a tag-merging helper that computes the rewrite's final tag set from existing tags + newly-computed Influx-owned tags, so that rewrites follow the ownership contract in master PRD §8.1 (FR-NOTE-5, FR-NOTE-6, FR-NOTE-7, FR-NOTE-8).
acceptanceCriteria: src/influx/notes.py exposes a tag-merging helper (exact API shape left to the implementation) that accepts the parsed existing tags and the newly-computed Influx-owned tags and returns the final merged tag set for the rewrite

The helper fully replaces any existing tag matching the Influx-owned prefixes/values: `source:*`, `arxiv-id:*`, `cat:*`, `text:*`, `ingested-by:*`, `schema:*`, `full-text`, `influx:repair-needed`, `influx:archive-missing`, `influx:deep-extracted`, `influx:text-terminal` (FR-NOTE-5)
`profile:*` tags are merged as a union of existing and new (FR-NOTE-6)
When a per-profile `influx:rejected:<profile>` tag is present, the helper MUST NOT re-add the corresponding `profile:<profile>` tag (FR-NOTE-6) — a unit test asserts this guard

Tags authored outside Influx (anything not matching the Influx-owned list above and not `profile:*`) are preserved verbatim (FR-NOTE-7) — a unit test asserts external tags like `favourite` or `reading-queue` survive a rewrite

A confidence-recompute helper (or documented convention) computes `max(existing_confidence, current_max_score / 10.0)` (FR-NOTE-8) and a unit test covers both branches (existing > new, and new > existing)
tests/unit/test_notes_render.py or a dedicated tag-merging test file covers all of FR-NOTE-5/6/7/8 with explicit positive and negative cases
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement notes.py Archive section render and parse (FR-NOTE-9)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/notes.py` to render and parse the `## Archive` section per the exact contract so that archive paths live in exactly one authoritative location and parsers reject malformed bodies (FR-NOTE-9, AC-04-B).
acceptanceCriteria: The renderer places the `## Archive` section immediately after `# {Title}` and before any other Influx-owned section (FR-NOTE-9)
When an archive path is present, the `## Archive` section contains exactly one line of the form `path: <rel-path>` using POSIX separators, relative to `archive_root` (FR-NOTE-9)

When an archive path is absent, the renderer emits the `## Archive` section with an empty body (the canonical no-path form used by this PRD's failure path); the parser additionally accepts an omitted `## Archive` section as a legal state (FR-NOTE-9)
The renderer never writes both a `path:` line AND an `influx:archive-missing` tag on the same note — a unit test asserts this invariant is enforced (FR-NOTE-9)
The parser accepts both legal `## Archive` body states (non-empty `path: <rel-path>`, or empty body / omitted section) and returns the parsed path or None
The parser rejects (raises) a note whose `## Archive` section contains stray text that is neither empty nor a single `path:` line (AC-04-B)
No frontmatter field, alternative tag, or alternative body location carries the archive path — a unit test asserts that only `## Archive` is consulted by the parser and renderer (FR-NOTE-9)
tests/unit/test_notes_render.py and tests/unit/test_notes_parse.py together cover AC-04-B: non-empty `path:` round-trip, empty body round-trip, and malformed stray-text rejection
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement notes.py full canonical renderer with User Notes round-trip preservation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/notes.py` to expose a full canonical note renderer that composes frontmatter, title, `## Archive`, `## Summary`, `## Profile Relevance`, and `## User Notes` into a single byte-stable output, with `## User Notes` preserved byte-identically across parse → rewrite cycles (FR-NOTE-1..8, AC-04-A, R-5).
acceptanceCriteria: src/influx/notes.py exposes a full canonical renderer (exact API shape left to the implementation) that writes a complete note with: frontmatter including `note_type = "summary"`, `namespace = "influx"`, `source_url` (normalised canonical URL), the merged tag list from US-005, and `confidence` computed as follows — on initial note creation `confidence = max(profile_scores) / 10.0` (FR-NOTE-3); on rewrite `confidence = max(existing_confidence, current_max_score / 10.0)` per FR-NOTE-8 (reusing the US-005 confidence-recompute helper)

The renderer places `# {Title}` first, then `## Archive` immediately after the title and before any other Influx-owned section (FR-NOTE-9), then the Influx-owned body sections `## Summary` (from the Tier-1 enrich stub) and `## Profile Relevance` (relative order between these two sections is not constrained), and finally `## User Notes` (existing region preserved byte-exactly, or an empty one appended when absent per FR-NOTE-4)
On rewrite, a per-profile `## Profile Relevance` entry is NOT refreshed when `influx:rejected:<profile>` is set (FR-NOTE-6) — a unit test asserts this
All Influx-authored notes carry the `ingested-by:influx` tag (FR-RES-6)

tests/unit/test_user_notes_roundtrip.py covers AC-04-A: a note with a non-trivial `## User Notes` section (including blank lines, unicode, and nested markdown headings) is parsed, re-rendered with updated Influx-owned tags, and the byte-exact user region survives (R-5 mitigation)
A golden-file test (fixture in tests/fixtures/) backs the round-trip to catch regressions in section ordering or whitespace
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement storage.py archive path construction and path-safety check
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/storage.py` to construct the archive path for a given source/id and verify it cannot escape `archive_root`, so that a malicious or malformed identifier cannot write outside the archive tree (FR-ST-1, FR-ST-2, FR-ST-3, AC-04-C).
acceptanceCriteria: src/influx/storage.py exposes a path-construction helper (exact API shape left to the implementation) that produces, for a given source, id, and published date, (a) a filesystem path under `archive_root / {source}/{YYYY}/{MM}/{id}.{ext}` (FR-ST-1) and (b) a note-facing relative path `{source}/{YYYY}/{MM}/{id}.{ext}` using POSIX separators, suitable for the `## Archive` section's `path:` line (FR-NOTE-9)
For arXiv, the path uses `source = "arxiv"`, extension `.pdf`, and the arXiv ID as-is — the arXiv ID is globally unique so no `{url-hash}` is appended (FR-ST-1)
Feed slugs used anywhere in the path satisfy `^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$`, max 40 chars — the helper calls into the existing `slugs.py` from PRD 01 rather than re-implementing the regex (FR-ST-2)
After building the full filesystem archive path, the helper verifies `path.resolve().is_relative_to(archive_root)` and rejects (raises) otherwise (FR-ST-3)

tests/unit/test_archive_path.py covers AC-04-C: an arXiv ID containing `..` (e.g. `../../etc/passwd`) and an absolute-path injection are both rejected BEFORE any download is attempted (additional hardening such as trailing-slash or encoded-traversal attempts is optional and may be included at the implementer's discretion, but is not required by AC-04-C)
A positive test confirms a well-formed arXiv ID produces the expected filesystem path under `archive_root` and the expected POSIX-separator note-facing relative path `{source}/{YYYY}/{MM}/{id}.{ext}`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement storage.py archive download with guarded HTTP client and failure-path tagging
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/storage.py` to download the archive PDF via the guarded HTTP client from PRD 02, and on any download failure emit the canonical failure signal (empty `## Archive` body + `influx:repair-needed` + `influx:archive-missing` tags) so that PRD 06's repair sweep can pick it up (FR-ST-4, AC-X-4 partial).
acceptanceCriteria: The download helper uses the guarded HTTP client from PRD 02 (SSRF guard, oversize abort via `storage.max_download_bytes`, timeouts) rather than a direct `httpx`/`urllib` call

On successful download, the PDF is written to the path produced by the US-008 helper and the returned archive metadata includes the relative POSIX path suitable for the `## Archive` section's `path:` line

On download failure (SSRF rejection, timeout, oversize abort, HTTP error, or any archive-step failure), the helper returns a signal that the caller uses to render the note with an EMPTY `## Archive` body (no `path:` line), tagged with BOTH `influx:repair-needed` AND `influx:archive-missing` (FR-ST-4)

A unit test simulates oversize abort (exceeding `storage.max_download_bytes` mid-stream) and asserts the failure signal is returned and drives the empty `## Archive` body plus `influx:repair-needed` and `influx:archive-missing` tags (AC-X-4 partial setup)
A unit test simulates SSRF rejection and asserts the failure signal is returned

Archive-step failures (SSRF rejection, timeout, oversize abort, HTTP error, or any other archive-step failure) are surfaced by `storage.py` in a form that callers can detect and handle — the implementation is free to use the existing typed-exception model (e.g. `NetworkError` from `src/influx/errors.py`) or any other mechanism, as long as the observable behaviour is that the caller can render the note with an empty `## Archive` body plus `influx:repair-needed` and `influx:archive-missing` tags and continue processing the next item; the run-level assertion that the whole run continues across per-item failures (FR-RES-1) is owned by US-015 at the `run_profile` wire-up layer
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement sources/arxiv.py Atom fetcher with client-side date filtering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/sources/arxiv.py` to query `https://export.arxiv.org/api/query` for configured categories, parse the Atom response with stdlib `xml.etree`, and apply client-side date filtering against `profile.sources.arxiv.lookback_days`, so that profiles receive only recent items per FR-SRC-1 and FR-SRC-2.
acceptanceCriteria: src/influx/sources/__init__.py is created

src/influx/sources/arxiv.py exposes a fetcher (exact API shape left to the implementation) that queries `https://export.arxiv.org/api/query` using the FR-SRC-1 parameter contract: `search_query` built as an OR-joined expression (`cat:X+OR+cat:Y+...`) from `profile.sources.arxiv.categories` (the existing `ArxivSourceConfig.categories: list[str]` in `src/influx/config.py`), `sortBy=submittedDate`, `sortOrder=descending`, and `max_results=profile.sources.arxiv.max_results_per_category` (FR-SRC-1)
The fetcher parses the Atom response using stdlib `xml.etree` (no new parsing dependency)
Date filtering is client-side on `<published>` parsed as UTC, against `profile.sources.arxiv.lookback_days` — items older than the lookback are dropped (FR-SRC-2)

On arXiv HTTP 429, the fetcher sleeps `resilience.arxiv_429_backoff_seconds` before retry (FR-RES-2); general retries use exponential backoff from `resilience.backoff_base_seconds` (e.g. `base`, `base*2`, `base*4`, …) with max retries default 3 (FR-RES-1)
tests/fixtures/arxiv/*.atom contains 3–5 recorded Atom fixtures at different dates

tests/unit/test_arxiv_fetcher.py drives the fetcher against those fixtures (no live network), asserting: correct query URL construction, Atom parsing extracts arXiv id / title / abstract / published / categories, lookback filtering drops older items, and 429 backoff is honoured
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Internal request-grouping strategy (one grouped request or multiple per-category requests) is an implementation choice and not constrained by the acceptance criteria, provided the FR-SRC-1 query-parameter contract is observed on the wire.

userStories.title: Add arXiv global rate limiter and run-scoped shared-fetch cache
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a module- or service-level rate limiter that enforces `resilience.arxiv_request_min_interval_seconds` GLOBALLY across a run (not just within one caller) AND a run-scoped shared-fetch cache around the arXiv fetcher so that repeated requests for the same category within one run resolve to a single underlying HTTP call, so that we respect arXiv rate limits, avoid redundant work, and satisfy AC-04-E cross-profile de-duplication within this PRD (FR-SRC-3, AC-04-D, AC-04-E, R-8).
acceptanceCriteria: A rate limiter (exact API shape left to the implementation) enforces a minimum interval of `resilience.arxiv_request_min_interval_seconds` (default 3s) between successive requests to `export.arxiv.org`, applied globally across the run rather than per-caller (FR-SRC-3, R-8)

Within a single run, repeated requests for the same arXiv category resolve to a single underlying HTTP call and all callers receive equivalent parsed results — exact mechanism (cache, context, wrapper, etc.) is left to the implementation (AC-04-E, R-8)

tests/unit/test_arxiv_fetcher.py covers AC-04-D: three sequential fetches observe an inter-request gap of at least `resilience.arxiv_request_min_interval_seconds` (use a fake clock or sleep-monkeypatch so the test is fast and deterministic)

A test covers AC-04-E: within a single run, when two profiles share an arXiv category, the run-scoped shared-fetch layer produces exactly one underlying HTTP call for that category and both profiles receive equivalent parsed results
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Codebase-constrained proxy for AC-04-E: current `/runs` is single-profile (see `src/influx/http_api.py`) and `src/influx/scheduler.py` already operates per profile in-process, so AC-04-E's two-profiles-in-one-run observable is exercised in this PRD by driving the run-scoped shared-fetch API with the two-profile call pattern directly rather than via a multi-profile `/runs` endpoint. Full multi-profile `/runs` fan-out is owned by PRD 09 and will reuse the same shared-fetch API.

userStories.title: Implement filter.py prompt rendering with negative examples
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/filter.py` to render the filter prompt from `prompts.filter` with the required variables `{profile_description}`, `{negative_examples}`, and `{min_score_in_results}`, so that the LLM sees the correct context before scoring (FR-FLT-1, FR-FLT-5).
acceptanceCriteria: src/influx/filter.py exposes a prompt-rendering helper (exact API shape left to the implementation) that substitutes `{profile_description}`, `{negative_examples}`, and `{min_score_in_results}` into the template loaded from `prompts.filter` (FR-FLT-1)

Negative examples are rendered one per line as `- "{title}" (rejected)`, with each title truncated to `filter.negative_example_max_title_chars`, and the total list capped at `feedback.negative_examples_per_profile` (FR-FLT-5)

When the negative-examples input list is empty (the PRD-04 case — PRD 05 wires `lithos_list` for real feedback), the rendered `{negative_examples}` substitution is empty (no negative-example lines emitted and no invented placeholder text) so prompt semantics are not altered beyond what the PRD authorises

tests/unit/test_filter.py covers: all three variables substituted; title longer than `filter.negative_example_max_title_chars` is truncated; more-than-cap negative examples are truncated to the cap; empty negative-examples list is handled
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement filter.py LLM call with JSON-mode validation, retries, and batching
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/filter.py` to call `models.filter` in JSON mode, validate the response against `FilterResponse`, retry on parse failure up to `models.filter.max_retries`, and batch items at `filter.batch_size`, so that scoring is robust under flaky LLM output (FR-FLT-2, FR-FLT-3, FR-FLT-4, FR-FLT-6, AC-04-F, AC-04-G).
acceptanceCriteria: A JSON-mode-capable LLM client permitted by PRD §7 (`litellm` or a direct provider SDK such as OpenAI or Anthropic) is added as a dependency in pyproject.toml and resolves on install
The filter caller invokes `models.filter` with `response_format={"type": "json_object"}` when `json_mode = true` (FR-FLT-2)
Responses are validated against `FilterResponse` from US-002 (FR-FLT-3)
Items are batched at `filter.batch_size` (default 25) (FR-FLT-4)
On JSON-mode parse failure (invalid JSON, schema violation, or any validation error), the filter logs the raw response and retries within `models.filter.max_retries` (FR-FLT-6)

When retries are exhausted, the filter yields zero validated results for that batch and logs the skip — NO regex salvage is attempted (FR-FLT-6); the end-to-end no-partial-write observable at the filter-to-render boundary (AC-04-G) is owned by US-015

tests/unit/test_filter.py asserts that the filter produces one validated `FilterResult` per input item when the LLM returns well-formed JSON-mode responses (threshold-based acceptance filtering is owned by US-014; the end-to-end filter-to-render call-count observable for AC-04-F is owned by US-015)

tests/unit/test_filter.py asserts JSON-mode retry-and-skip behaviour at the filter-internal level (this is NOT AC-04-G coverage — AC-04-G's no-partial-write observable is owned by US-015 at the filter-to-render boundary): a malformed JSON-mode response triggers retry up to `max_retries`, after which the batch is skipped and the filter yields zero validated results for that batch with a log entry
Pre-recorded filter responses keyed by input hash drive the deterministic filter test (no live LLM calls in CI)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement filter.py threshold behaviour and tier tagging (FR-FLT-7)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/filter.py` to apply per-profile thresholds (`relevance`, `full_text`, `deep_extract`, `notify_immediate`) to each scored item and emit the correct tier tags, so that downstream PRDs (06 repair sweep, 07 Tier 2/3 enrichment) see the intended signal shape (FR-FLT-7).
acceptanceCriteria: Score < `profile.thresholds.relevance` (default 7) → item is discarded (no note written) (FR-FLT-7)
Score ≥ `relevance` → item is kept for canonical-note rendering with Tier 1 sections (FR-FLT-7)
Score ≥ `full_text` → the item carries the `full-text` tag (FR-FLT-7)
Score ≥ `deep_extract` → the item carries the `influx:deep-extracted` tag (FR-FLT-7)
Score ≥ `notify_immediate` → the item is flagged for digest highlights (FR-FLT-7) — actual digest wiring is a stub in this PRD (PRD 05), so asserting the flag is sufficient

For items crossing `full_text` or `deep_extract`, the Tier 2 / Tier 3 BODY SECTIONS are NOT rendered in this PRD — only the corresponding TAG is written, and the item also carries `influx:repair-needed` so PRD 06's sweep picks it up for upgrade (FR-FLT-7 temporary-mismatch seam)

tests/unit/test_filter.py covers each threshold boundary (score = threshold − 1, = threshold, = threshold + 1) for `relevance`, `full_text`, `deep_extract`, and `notify_immediate`, asserting the correct combination of tags and the `influx:repair-needed` seam
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire run_profile to drive the end-to-end arXiv flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the existing `influx.scheduler.run_profile` hook (the PRD-03 stub called by `http_api.py`) replaced with the real arXiv flow — fetch (US-010/US-011) → filter (US-012/US-013/US-014) → per-accepted-item archive (US-008/US-009) + render note (US-007) → stub `lithos_client.write` (US-003) — with a dedup check via stub `lithos_client.cache_lookup`, so that `POST /runs` produces at least one stub-recorded write with the correct canonical shape (§2 "Wire a real run_profile", AC-M1-7, AC-M1-9, AC-M1-10).
acceptanceCriteria: The existing `influx.scheduler.run_profile` hook (the PRD-03 stub called by `http_api.py`) is wired to the real arXiv flow — calling the arXiv fetcher, filter, per-accepted-item archive, and note render in the correct order; the choice of which file owns the implementation is left to the implementer (PRD §6 lists `src/influx/service.py` as the file to modify for the wire-up)

Before rendering a note, the flow consults `lithos_client.cache_lookup` — when the stub returns "cached" the item is skipped (AC-M1-9 via unit test against the dedup call site); when it returns "not cached" (the default stub behaviour) the flow proceeds

After each accepted item is rendered, the flow calls `lithos_client.write` with the canonical note payload (recorded by the stub from US-003) — recorded writes include `source_url = https://arxiv.org/abs/<id>`, tags containing `arxiv-id:<id>`, `profile:<name>`, `source:arxiv`, `ingested-by:influx`, `schema:1`, `confidence = max_score / 10.0`, `namespace = "influx"`, `note_type = "summary"` (AC-M1-10)
The digest/webhook hook fires after the run but the actual HTTP POST is NOT required (stubbed here; real webhook is PRD 05) — a unit or integration test asserts the hook fires (AC-M1-7)
Per-item failures (download failure, filter batch skipped, etc.) do NOT abort the run (FR-RES-1)
A unit test against the dedup call site asserts that when the stub `cache_lookup` returns a "cached" response the item is skipped (AC-M1-9 contract surface)

A unit test at the filter-to-render boundary covers AC-04-F: when the filter is driven with a batch of 25 items and 3 are above `profile.thresholds.relevance`, the flow makes exactly 3 note-render invocations and exactly 3 stub-recorded `lithos_client.write` calls (no more, no fewer)

A unit test at the filter-to-render boundary covers AC-04-G: when a malformed JSON-mode filter response causes the batch to be skipped after `models.filter.max_retries` are exhausted, the flow performs ZERO note renders and ZERO stub-recorded `lithos_client.write` calls for that batch (no partial notes written), and the skip is logged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add integration test for end-to-end arXiv flow via POST /runs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `tests/integration/test_arxiv_flow.py` to drive `POST /runs {"profile": "<arXiv-enabled-profile>"}` end-to-end using recorded Atom fixtures (US-010) and pre-recorded filter responses (US-013), so that AC-M1-7 + AC-M1-10 + the repair-needed/archive-missing signals are asserted against stub-recorded `lithos_client.write` calls (§10 Definition of Done).
acceptanceCriteria: tests/integration/test_arxiv_flow.py exercises `POST /runs {"profile": "<arXiv-enabled-profile>"}` end-to-end against a running service (in-process or subprocess is an implementation choice) and waits for the run to complete
The test asserts the service returned `202` + `request_id` and that after completion at least one stub-recorded `lithos_client.write` call exists with the canonical shape from AC-M1-10
The test uses the recorded Atom fixtures from US-010 (no live network) and pre-recorded filter responses (no live LLM)

The test asserts `AC-M2-3` partial: for an item with only an abstract, the recorded note carries `text:abstract-only`, no Tier 2/3 body, both `influx:repair-needed` AND `influx:archive-missing` when applicable, and does NOT carry `influx:text-terminal`
The test asserts the digest/webhook hook fired without requiring a real HTTP POST (AC-M1-7)
Typecheck passes
